meta.description: US boxing promoters voice lines for procedurally generated regional promoters. Tone: direct, business-focused, American. The US promoter talks about money, opportunity, and leverage — a different register from the Latvian dry understatement. Named promoters like Bob Arum and Oscar De La Hoya are not hardcoded here — they are represented through the international named promoters in universal/promoters.json.

initialOffer: I've been watching {fighterName}. I've got a card in {city} next month and I want them on it. Here are the numbers.
Your fighter has a good look. I can get them on television. Let's talk money.
Got a slot at {weightClass} on our {city} card. Your fighter fits. What do you need?
I run the {city} market. {fighterName} is the kind of fighter I can do business with. Call me.
We need a {weightClass} fighter for a March card at {venue}. Your guy ready to step up?
I saw {fighterName} fight last month. I can move this kid. Here's what I'm offering.
offerDeclined: Your call. When you're ready to do business, you know where to find me.
Understood. Someone else will take the slot.
That's fine. Don't wait too long — this market moves fast.
OK. But I'll need an answer in 48 hours next time.
Alright. We'll talk when the timing works for you.
fightWon: Good performance. I've got bigger plans for this kid. Let's sit down.
Perfect. The right people were watching tonight. Follow up with me Monday.
That's what I needed to see. We're moving up.
Solid win. I can sell this fighter nationally now. Let's talk.
People noticed. That's the business. What do you want next?
fightLost: Tough night. It happens in this business. Let's talk about where we go from here.
It happens. The question is how your fighter responds. I'll be watching.
Not the result we wanted but I can still work with this. Call me.
These things happen. Get back in the gym, get back in the ring. This isn't over.
Boxing is a long game. We move forward.
contractOffer: I want to sign {fighterName} to a multi-fight deal. Here's what I'm offering — it's fair and I stand behind every number.
Ready to make this official. I want {fighterName} on my roster. Here's the contract.
Long-term relationship. I'm serious about building this fighter's career. Take a look at what I'm proposing.
I don't offer contracts to fighters I'm not serious about. Read this carefully.
We've done good business together. Time to formalise it. Here's the deal.
